summary: Colorectal cancer in individuals under 50 is often linked to inherited conditions such as Lynch or FAP, showing unique characteristics including right-sided tumor locations, mucin production, and microsatellite instability—features distinguishing them from common sporadic cases. This suggests these cancers represent a separate biological and clinical category rather than just early-onset versions of standard disease.
